claim: Bolt.new token limits and cost are problems; forking allows local deployment with any LLM including Ollama-based models for free unlimited usage.
actionable: Fork Bolt.new and run it locally with Ollama to eliminate token limits and API costs—extend the platform by modifying the constants file to add new models from different providers.
source_title: I Forked Bolt.new and Made it WAY Better

claim: Switching between LLMs in Bolt.new requires infrastructure changes; Cole's fork enables mid-conversation model switching by storing model selection in message content, then sanitizing before API calls.
actionable: When building multi-LLM chat interfaces, store model selection in user message content and sanitize before passing to LLM—this pattern bypasses limitations in streaming hooks from AI SDKs.
source_title: I Forked Bolt.new and Made it WAY Better

claim: Smaller models (7B parameters) struggle with Bolt.new's massive internal prompts, failing to spin up web containers reliably despite being fine-tuned for coding.
actionable: When using smaller models for full-stack generation, test context limits and be prepared to fall back to larger models or provide simplified prompts if web container initialization fails.
source_title: I Forked Bolt.new and Made it WAY Better

claim: Setting up local Ollama models for Bolt.new requires installing Chrome Canary browser, setting API keys for cloud providers, and running `pnpm run dev` with environment variables.
actionable: To run Bolt.new locally with Ollama: install Chrome Canary, clone the fork, set up .env variables (only for cloud providers you use), install Ollama models, then `pnpm run dev`.
source_title: I Forked Bolt.new and Made it WAY Better

claim: The Vercel AI SDK supports multiple providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Groq, Ollama, etc.); adding new providers requires implementing provider-specific functions in a switch statement.
actionable: To add a new provider to Bolt's backend, implement provider-specific model functions, add API key handling, and extend the switch statements in get-model and api-key TypeScript files.
source_title: I Forked Bolt.new and Made it WAY Better

claim: Deep Seek Coder 236B and Qwen 2.5 Coder 32B are powerful coding models available via Ollama but may outperform Claude 3.5 Sonnet depending on task complexity.
actionable: Test local coding models (Deep Seek 236B, Qwen 32B) against your cloud LLM baseline; they may offer better cost/performance for context-heavy tasks where context length matters.
source_title: I Forked Bolt.new and Made it WAY Better
